name: Couchbase
description: Multi-model NoSQL database combining document, key-value, and full-text search with SQL++ query language and mobile sync

license.comment: Business Source License 1.1; source-available but not OSI-approved. Converts to Apache 2.0 after 4 years

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted Community/Enterprise editions, Couchbase Capella (official managed DBaaS), available on AWS/Azure/GCP marketplaces

initial-release.comment: Formed from merger of Membase and CouchOne in February 2011; Couchbase Server 1.8 released January 2012

primary-language.comment: Core database engine written in C++ (derived from Memcached heritage)

data-model.comment: JSON documents with optional key-value access; also supports full-text search and vector search

topology.comment: Each vBucket has a primary and replicas; masterless client architecture where each client coordinates its own operations

sharding.comment: Uses 1024 vBuckets per bucket with CRC32 hash partitioning; automatic rebalancing when nodes added/removed

replication-factor.comment: Supports 0 to 3 replicas per bucket; default is 1 replica

multi-region.comment: Cross Data Center Replication (XDCR) enables active-active geo-distribution; Enterprise Edition feature only

cap-position.comment: Primarily CP with strong consistency on master reads; can be tuned toward AP with replica reads and XDCR eventual consistency

consistency-model.comment: Strong consistency by default with master reads; replica reads provide eventual consistency; XDCR uses eventual consistency

acid-transactions.comment: Full distributed multi-document ACID transactions across buckets, scopes, and collections

transaction-scope.comment: Distributed transactions can span multiple documents across different nodes, collections, scopes, and buckets

isolation-levels.comment: Provides Read Committed isolation with Monotonic Atomic View (MAV) semantics; prevents lost updates via CAS

distributed-transactions.comment: Client-coordinated distributed transactions with no central coordinator; each SDK handles its own transaction coordination

query-language.comment: SQL++ (formerly N1QL) - SQL-like query language extended for JSON documents

sql-compatibility.comment: SQL++ extends SQL with JSON-specific features; supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, JOINs, subqueries, but schema differences from traditional SQL

secondary-indexes.comment: Global Secondary Indexes (GSI) for efficient query execution; supports composite, array, and covering indexes

joins.comment: SQL++ supports INNER, LEFT OUTER, and NEST joins across documents and collections

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support including GROUP BY, COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, ARRAY_AGG

full-text-search.comment: Built-in FTS service based on Bleve; integrated with SQL++ queries; supports vector search since v7.6

max-cluster-size.comment: No documented hard limit; production deployments commonly use 10-100+ nodes; forum discussions mention 130+ nodes

write-throughput.comment: ~6000 writes/sec per node with SSD; ~1500 with 10K drives; scales linearly with nodes

read-latency-p99.comment: Sub-200us on 10G network, 500-900us on 1Gbps, 1-2ms on AWS for cached key-value operations

auto-scaling.comment: Capella provides auto-scaling; Kubernetes Operator supports horizontal pod autoscaling; self-hosted requires manual rebalance

hot-spot-handling.comment: vBuckets distributed evenly across nodes with automatic rebalancing during topology changes

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling online upgrades supported; swap rebalance allows upgrading without capacity reduction

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-less JSON documents; fields can be added/removed without migration; indexes can be created online

point-in-time-recovery.comment: PITR is in Developer Preview; production relies on scheduled incremental backups and CDC

backup-strategy.comment: cbbackupmgr provides full and incremental backups; Backup Service supports scheduled backups with AWS S3 integration

kubernetes-operator.comment: Couchbase Autonomous Operator; certified for OpenShift, EKS, AKS, GKE, and Rancher

rust-driver.comment: Official community-supported Rust SDK in couchbaselabs; currently in development, not production-ready

java-driver.comment: Official Couchbase Java SDK with sync and reactive APIs

python-driver.comment: Official Couchbase Python SDK with asyncio and Twisted support

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official C++ SDK (libcouchbase); also serves as core for Python, Ruby, PHP, and Node.js SDKs

orm-support.comment: Spring Data Couchbase available for Java; no major ORM support for traditional ORMs due to document model

open-source.comment: BSL 1.1 is not OSI-approved; considered source-available. Community Edition has cluster size restrictions

free-tier.comment: Capella Free Tier launched September 2024; perpetual free tier for learning/development, no credit card required

pricing-model.comment: Capella uses credits based on compute, storage, IOPS, and region; Enterprise license for self-hosted

cost-estimate-small.comment: Estimate for 3-node basic Capella cluster; varies by region, compute specs, and storage

change-data-capture.comment: Database Change Protocol (DCP) provides streaming changes; CDC capabilities for Magma storage since v7.2.2

watch-subscribe.comment: Eventing Service provides OnUpdate/OnDelete handlers for document mutations in real-time

triggers.comment: Eventing Service executes JavaScript functions on document mutations and timers

event-streaming.comment: Official Kafka Connect Couchbase connector for source and sink; Confluent-verified

github-stars.comment: Couchbase Server uses multi-repo structure; individual SDK repos have modest star counts (52-277 stars)

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Approximate count as of December 2025; related tags include n1ql, spring-data-couchbase, couchbase-lite
